title: Accessibility-First: How We Design and Build Inclusive Components
content:
## Accessibility as a constraint, not an afterthought

Every component in the Green Design System is designed with accessibility as a primary constraint. This means WCAG 2.2 AA conformance is a release requirement, not a nice-to-have. If a component doesn't meet the bar, it doesn't ship.

### Keyboard navigation

All interactive components are fully keyboard-navigable following established ARIA patterns. We use the roving tabindex technique for composite widgets like menus and dropdown lists, ensuring that keyboard users can navigate efficiently without excessive Tab key presses.

### Screen reader testing

Each component is tested with VoiceOver on macOS/iOS and NVDA on Windows before release. We maintain a test matrix that covers critical user journeys — form completion, modal dialogs, data tables — across the two most common screen reader and browser combinations.

### Focus management

Dialogs and other overlay components implement the ARIA authoring practices for focus trapping and restoration. When a dialog opens, focus moves to the first interactive element inside it. When it closes, focus returns to the trigger that opened it.
